title: Pleurothallis dayana
intro: Pleurothallis dayana, a species of orchid, is a type of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis dayana include flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Pleurothallis dayana is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This orchid species is further categorized under the subfamily Epidendroideae. Pleurothallis dayana is a member of the tribe Maxillarieae. Within this tribe, it is a part of the subtribe Cymbidiinae. The exact classification within the genus Pleurothallis is not well-documented, but it is generally accepted as a species within this genus.
